exemplar.title: To what extent has Lego been successful in incorporating sustainability as the key element in their production strategies?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Lego been successful in incorporating sustainability as the key element in their production strategies?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistently centers sustainability in major sections
Sound discussion of trade-offs (e.g., PET energy vs. recyclability)
Strong application of sustainability concept to packaging transition
weaknesses: Several descriptive passages lack an explicit sustainability lens
Introduction and some STEEPLE descriptions omit direct conceptual framing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 4: the key concept of sustainability is kept at the centre of analysis and repeatedly linked to Legoâs initiatives. Discussion of trade-offs demonstrates analytical thinking beyond description. However, the concept is not woven into every paragraph and a few sections (e.g., injection moulding, methodology) lack explicit integration, preventing a band 5.

strengths: Includes five supporting documents matching the 3â5 requirement
Documents from Legoâs reports are directly relevant and supply useful quantitative data
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on internal Lego sources limits the range of perspectives
Some images serve illustrative rather than analytical purposes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission provides the required number (five) of supporting documents, all of which are relevant and sufficiently in-depth for band 3. Depth is acceptable but not wide-ranging; a broader mix of external sources would strengthen range and critical depth, so band 4 is not warranted.

strengths: Selection of STEEPLE and circular-economy frameworks suits the topic
Tools are applied to core sustainability initiatives (renewable energy, PET recycling)
weaknesses: Choice of tools is limited (no financial or strategic models)
Applications sometimes devolve into description without linking back to âsuccessâ metrics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While appropriate tools (STEEPLE, circular economy) are selected and applied, the range is narrow and some applications are descriptive. This aligns with band 2 (some tools selected, superficial relevance) rather than the more varied and clearly applied band 3.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Accurate use of key quantitative data (revenue, plastic tonnage, landfill volumes)
Genuine analytical effort in discussing waste reduction, packaging trade-offs, and regional variability
weaknesses: Reliance on narrative descriptions without ratio/trend calculations
Evaluation is intermittent and largely qualitative, lacking sustained integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analysis are adequate (band 3): key figures are used correctly and discussion shows evaluation of trade-offs. However, the analysis stops short of thorough, sustained argumentation and deeper quantitative critique, so band 4 is not yet achieved.

strengths: Conclusions directly reflect evidence from analysis sections
Explicitly answers the research question with balanced judgment on achievements and limitations
weaknesses: Could reference specific data points to strengthen claims
Limited discussion of broader implications or recommendations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fully aligns with the evidence and addresses the research question explicitly, fulfilling band 3 criteria. A more detailed tie-back to key metrics would be needed for band 4.

strengths: IB-prescribed structure is followed (introduction, tools, conclusion, references)
Clear table of contents and logical flow throughout
weaknesses: Methodology paragraph ends abruptly, suggesting incomplete description
Research question could include measurable indicators for focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization and structure are appropriate throughout (band 2). Minor lapses in the methodology section prevent a perfect score, but overall sequencing and headings are coherent.

strengths: All required elements are present (title page, RQ, word count, TOC, citations, references)
Supporting documents are numbered and included
weaknesses: Inconsistent list numbering and empty table reduce professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All formal presentation requirements are met (band 2). Formatting inconsistencies and incomplete table data limit polish but do not detract from completeness of required elements.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the prescribed IB structure (introduction, tools, conclusion, references) and includes a clear table of contents.  Headings and logical flow indicate **appropriate structure throughout**.

Figures from the sustainability report, landfill data, and an infographic on PET bricks are clearly pertinent to the RQ and provide numbers that can be interrogated.  Depth is **sufficient** but not wide-ranging; most documents come from Legoâs own report, so the range of perspectives is limited and some images are only illustrative.
All required formal elementsâtitle page with RQ and word count, table of contents, citations, reference list, and numbered supporting documentsâare present.
Five separate supporting documents are listed and reproduced, matching the required range of 3-5.

STEEPLE factors are applied to Legoâs renewable-energy investments and regional supply-chain moves; the circular-economy tool is used to evaluate PET recycling and its implications.  Relevance is generally clear, but some applications remain descriptive (e.g., listing injection-moulding steps) and do not always link explicitly back to âsuccessâ in sustainability, so the application is **adequate but uneven**.

Revenue, tonnage of plastic, landfill volumes and packaging percentages are selected and quoted correctly, giving a factual basis for discussion.  The range is moderate and figures are used in narrative form rather than through calculated trends or ratios; thus data use is **adequate but not extensive**.

The student analyses progress in waste reduction and sustainable packaging, notes trade-offs (energy use for PET), and weighs cost implications.  These elements show genuine analytical effort, yet evaluation is **intermittent** and largely qualitative, stopping short of a sustained, integrated argument.

The research questionââto what extent has Lego been successfulâ¦ââis addressed directly in the first line of the conclusion, with balanced judgement on achievements and limitations, thereby **explicitly answering** the RQ.

The student selects STEEPLE and the Circular Economy frameworkâboth appropriate for an environmental production issueâand supplements them with limited quantitative data.  While adequate, the choice is **not especially varied** (e.g., no ratio analysis, BCG, etc.), so the selection is judged âadequateâ rather than âeffectiveâ.
The conclusion reiterates the main findings (successes in waste and material innovation; ongoing ABS issue) and aligns with evidence cited in the analysis sections, showing **full consistency**.

Legoâs context is developed through revenue trends, factory locations, specific material volumes (100 000 t plastic; 87 600 t packaging) and strategic statements from senior managers.  The STEEPLE and circular-economy sections apply these facts directly to the company, demonstrating **strong analysis** of how external factors and internal initiatives interact.  Yet the connection is not fully sustainedâe.g., the general explanation of injection moulding adds little analytical valueâso integration is solid but not comprehensive.

The student keeps the idea of sustainability at the centre of every major section, repeatedly linking ABS, PET and the circular-economy prototype back to the definition of sustainability given in the introduction.  Discussion of trade-offs (e.g., extra energy for drying PET) shows **sound conceptual understanding** and goes beyond a basic description.  However, the concept is not woven into *every* paragraphâseveral descriptive passages about production stages and STEEPLE factors omit an explicit conceptual lensâso the analysis is **partially rather than fully integrated**.

annotations.comment: The methodology paragraph ends abruptly, suggesting missing text. Ensure full description of your analytical steps to clarify your approach.
The technical description of ABS plastic use remains largely descriptive; integrate sustainability trade-offs (energy, recyclability) into this section.
The list of injection-molding steps is accurate but adds limited analytical value; consider summarizing and focusing on sustainability implications.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The STEEPLE framework is introduced, but only social and environmental factors are developed; either justify this focus or include political, economic, technological, legal and ethical dimensions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The environmental section presents key figures on renewable energy and plastic volumes, but lacks critical evaluationâdiscuss implications of these figures on long-term sustainability.
The social analysis describes factory locations and solar initiatives but needs deeper evaluation of how local communities and stakeholders are impacted.
Reliance on a VP quote adds authority but isnât critically assessedâconsider weighing stated progress against measurable outcomes.
The PET prototype development is well described; strengthen by analyzing quantitative test results and performance metrics.
The circular economy definition is generic; explicitly link each principle back to Legoâs PET initiative to demonstrate relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated but could specify measurable indicators of âsuccessâ to guide a more focused analysis.
Key Concept âSustainabilityâ is stated prominently; consider defining its dimensions (environmental, social, economic) to frame subsequent analysis.
The table of contents demonstrates clear organization with all major sections and supporting documents listed, enhancing navigability.
The introduction provides context and revenue figures but lacks an explicit link back to the sustainability conceptâreinforce how these facts support your conceptual lens.
The circular-economy figure is included but not interpreted in text; discuss its elements to reinforce your analytical argument.
The description of closing the loop outlines good circular practice; explicitly tie these steps back to your research question on LEGOâs overall sustainability success.
The list numbering begins at â2,â which disrupts readability; ensure consistent list formatting to maintain professionalism.
The Harvard Business School perspective is noted but requires deeper critique of its implications (e.g., energy trade-offs) to enrich evaluation.
Figure 2âs trend is described qualitatively; strengthen analysis by calculating and commenting on the rate of waste reduction.
The table of sustainable packaging targets is clear; add commentary on the significance of incremental changes between years.
Figure 3âs table is empty of data and should be completed or removed to avoid confusion.
The discussion of regional packaging challenges demonstrates nuanced analysis of external variabilityâwell linked to sustainability complexities.
The packaging transition narrative is well linked to evidence on material volumes, reinforcing the sustainability narrative.
The conclusion addresses the research question explicitly but would benefit from referencing key data points to substantiate judgments.
Some reference entries lack consistent format (e.g., missing retrieval dates or publisher), which reduces credibility.
Multiple sources are merged in one entry; separate each reference clearly and ensure full bibliographic details.
You include a good range of additional sources, but consider integrating their insights directly into your analysis sections.
